zh.lifeSummary: 當Isabella de St. Armand於1307年出生在英國越可爾赫爾時，她的父親John de St Amand26歲，母親Margaret le Despenser18歲。她於1330年12月16日與Sir Richard Hadlow在英國越可爾赫爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1361年10月在英國格盧斯特斯爾希勒斯利享年54歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Isabella de St. Armand于1307年出生在英国越可爾赫爾时，她的父亲John de St Amand26岁，母亲Margaret le Despenser18岁。她于1330年12月16日与Sir Richard Hadlow在英国越可爾赫爾结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1361年10月在英国格盧斯特斯爾希勒斯利享年54岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: St.
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Americanized spelling of French   St  .   Denis  .

variants.name: St
meanings: 1  English (of Norman origin): habitational name from any of the numerous places in France so called from the dedication of their churches to St. Jean (  see  John  ).
2  Americanized form of French   St  .   Jean  .

variants.name: Denis
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Camille, Jacques, Armand, Marcel, Andre, Pierre, Serge, Yvan, Alphonse, Edouard, Francois, Gaetane.  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Jorge, Roberto, Angel, Francisco, Joaquin, Juan, Manuel, Miguel, Ramon, Rolando.

French, Walloon, Breton, West Indian (mainly Haiti), German, Dutch, English, and Portuguese; Spanish and Galician (Denís): from the personal name Denis, Spanish and Galician Denís, from Latin Dionysius ‘(follower) of Dionysos’ (see Dennis ). Compare Dennee .
Ukrainian and Rusyn: variant transliteration of   Denys  , a cognate of 1 above.

nameMeanings.name: Armand
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French/Haitian Pierre, Lucien, Philippe, Serge, Alain, Andre, Andree, Camille, Etienne, Francoise, Gabrielle, Gardy.

French, West Indian (mainly Haiti), and Mauritian: from the French personal name Armand, from ancient Germanic Hardman or Hariman, composed of the elements hard ‘hard, strong’ or hari ‘army’ and man ‘man’ (see Hermann ). This surname is also found in England, where it is of Huguenot origin. Compare Arman , Arment , and Armond .
History: This surname is listed in the register of Huguenot ancestors recognized by the Huguenot Society of South Carolina.

variants.name: Arman
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Michel.
French: variant of   Armand   and, in North America, probably also an altered form of this.

Americanized form of German Armann: nickname for a impoverished person, Middle High German ar(m)mann ‘poor man’. There has been some confusion with the more common medieval ancient Germanic personal name Heriman ‘army man’ (see Hermann ).

variants.name: Hermann
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Hans, Jurgen, Mathias, Gottlieb, Horst, Juergen, Klaus, Lorenz, Bernd, Dieter, Erwin.

German: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements heri, hari ‘army’ + man ‘man’. This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Hungary, Poland, Czechia, Slovakia, and some other European countries; see also below. Compare Harman 5.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): adoption of German name (see 1 above). Compare   Herman  .

variants.name: Arment
meanings: Altered form of French   Armand  .
In some cases possibly also northern French: metonymic occupational name for a herdsman, from Old French arment ‘cattle, livestock’ (from Latin armenta ‘herds’). This surname is very rare in France.

variants.name: Armond
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Berard, Celina, Pierre.
French: variant of Armand and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this. The surname Armond is very rare in France, found mainly in the southeast.
English: from an altered form of the French personal name   Armand  .

variants.name: Armando
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Navarro, Alvarado, Andrade, Armando, Diaz, Emiliano, Garcia, Jimenez, Jorge, Martinez, Ramon, Sanchez.  Italian Angelo, Vito.
Italian, Spanish, and Portuguese: from Armando, a personal name of ancient Germanic origin, cognate with French Armand .

nameMeanings.name: Isabella
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   Isabel  , which has been popular in England since the 18th century and has recently become the preferred form of the name.

variants.name: Isobella
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Isabella de St. Armand was born in 1307, in Hull, Yorkshire, England, her father, John de St Amand, was 26 and her mother, Margaret le Despenser, was 18. She married Sir Richard Hadlow on 16 December 1330, in Hull, Yorkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. She died in October 1361, in Hillesley, Gloucestershire, England, at the age of 54.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Isabella de St. Armand est née en 1307, à Hull, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, John de St Amand, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Margaret le Despenser, avait 18 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Richard Hadlow le 16 décembre 1330, à Hull, Everwyck, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en octobre 1361, à Hillesley, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 54 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Isabella de St. Armand 1307 in Hull, Yorkshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John de St Amand 26 und ihre Mutter Margaret le Despenser 18. Sie hat am 16. Dezember 1330 in Hull, Yorkshire, England Sir Richard Hadlow geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Sie starb im Oktober 1361 im Alter von 54 Jahren in Hillesley, Gloucestershire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Isabella de St. Armand è nata il nel 1307 nel Hull, Yorkshire, England, sua padre, John de St Amand, aveva 26 anni e sua madre, Margaret le Despenser, aveva 18 anni. Lei sposato Sir Richard Hadlow il 16 dicembre 1330, nel Hull, Yorkshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. Morì nel ottobre 1361, nel Hillesley, Gloucestershire, England, all'età di 54 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Isabella de St. Armandが1307年にイギリスヨークシュアイアハルで生まれた時，父John de St Amandは26歳，母Margaret le Despenserは18歳であった。1330年12月16日にイギリスヨークシュアイアハルでSir Richard Hadlowと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1361年10月にイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアヒレスリーで54歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Isabella de St. Armand(이)가 1307년 영국 요르크실르 훌에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John de St Amand(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret le Despenser(은)는 18세였다. 그녀는 1330년 12월 16일 영국 요르크실르 훌에서 Sir Richard Hadlow(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1361년 10월 영국 그루스스터실르 힐레슬리에서 54세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Isabella de St. Armand nasceu em 1307, em Hull, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, John de St Amand, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Margaret le Despenser, tinha 18 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Richard Hadlow em 16 de dezembro de 1330, em Hull, Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em outubro de 1361, em Hillesley, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, com 54 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Isabella de St. Armand родилась в 1307 г. в местности хулл, еркшир, Англия, её отцу по имени John de St Amand было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret le Despenser, 18. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Richard Hadlow 16 декабря 1330 г. в местности хулл, еркшир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Она умерла октябрь 1361 г. в местности хиллесли, глоусестиршир, Англия в возрасте 54 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Isabella de St. Armand nació en 1307, en Hull, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, su papá, John de St Amand, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Margaret le Despenser, tenía 18 años. Se casó con Sir Richard Hadlow el 16 de diciembre de 1330, en Hull, Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas. Murió en octubre de 1361, en Hillesley, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 54 años.
